text: Working with APIs allows developers to connect different software applications. An API uses HTTP requests, such as GET or POST, to communicate. When you send a request, the server often returns data in JSON format, which is easy for programs to read. Many APIs require authentication, usually through an API key, to ensure only authorised users access the data. It is also important to be aware of rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make in a given time. Finally, always include error handling in your code. This helps you manage problems like server errors or invalid requests smoothly, making your application more reliable.
